perturbation.original_text: 6. Except as aforementioned, the receiving Party shall have no obligations or restrictions with respect to any Proprietary Information which that receiving Party can prove: DEFTA Purchasing department 2016 ANNEX 2 - Non Disclosure Agreement (a) has come into the public domain prior to, or after the disclosure thereof and in such case through no wrongful act of tha t receiving Party, or (b) is already known to the receiving Party, as evidenced by written documentation in the files of the receiving Party, or (c) has been lawfully received from a third party without restrictions or breach of this Agreement, or (d) has been or is published without violation of this Agreement, or (e) is independently developed in good faith by an employee or employees of that receiving Party who did not have access to t he Proprietary Information, or (f) is approved for release or use by written authorization of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The receiving Party shall always have obligations and restrictions with respect to any Proprietary Information, even if that receiving Party can prove: DEFTA Purchasing department 2016 ANNEX 2 - Non Disclosure Agreement (a) has come into the public domain prior to, or after the disclosure thereof and in such case through no wrongful act of tha t receiving Party, or (b) is already known to the receiving Party, as evidenced by written documentation in the files of the receiving Party, or (c) has been lawfully received from a third party without restrictions or breach of this Agreement, or (d) has been or is published without violation of this Agreement, or (e) is independently developed in good faith by an employee or employees of that receiving Party who did not have access to t he Proprietary Information, or (f) is approved for release or use by written authorization of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause provides exceptions to the receiving party's obligations regarding Proprietary Information under specific circumstances (e.g., information in the public domain). The changed clause asserts that the receiving party always has obligations and restrictions, even if those circumstances are met, which introduces a perturbation by negating all the exceptions listed in the subsequent sub-clauses. This creates an in-text contradiction because the exceptions described are now meaningless. This introduces a perturbation because there is a contradiction of the rules and obligations of the receiving party, making the exceptions null and void.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The receiving Party shall always have obligations and restrictions with respect to any Proprietary Information, even if that receiving Party can prove: DEFTA Purchasing department 2016 ANNEX 2 - Non Disclosure Agreement (a) has come into the public domain prior to, or after the disclosure thereof and in such case through no wrongful act of tha t receiving Party, or (b) is already known to the receiving Party, as evidenced by written documentation in the files of the receiving Party, or (c) has been lawfully received from a third party without restrictions or breach of this Agreement, or (d) has been or is published without violation of this Agreement, or (e) is independently developed in good faith by an employee or employees of that receiving Party who did not have access to t he Proprietary Information, or (f) is approved for release or use by written authorization of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text asserts that the receiving party 'shall always have obligations and restrictions', directly contradicting the original text's provision of exceptions. This creates an in-text contradiction because the exceptions listed in sub-clauses (a) through (f) become meaningless since they no longer provide valid reasons for exemption from obligations, according to the new clause. The contradicted text in the prompt is an exact copy of the changed text, and it serves to highlight the self-contradictory nature of the modification.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Except as provided under clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions, it is expressly understood and agreed by the Par ties hereto that the disclosure and provision of Proprietary Information under this Agreement by a Party to the other Party shall notbe construed as granting to the receiving Party under the laws of any country any rights whether expressed or implied by licence or otherwise on the matters, inventions or discoveries to which such Proprietary Information pertains or any copyright, trademar k or trade secret rights. The property in all information and/or data disclosed by the Buyer to the Supplier pursuant to this Agreement shall subject t o any right of any other owner, rest with the Buyer.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Even if clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions are in place, it is expressly understood and agreed by the Parties hereto that the disclosure and provision of Proprietary Information under this Agreement by a Party to the other Party shall be construed as granting to the receiving Party under the laws of any country rights whether expressed or implied by licence or otherwise on the matters, inventions or discoveries to which such Proprietary Information pertains including any copyright, trademark or trade secret rights. The property in all information and/or data disclosed by the Buyer to the Supplier pursuant to this Agreement shall subject t o any right of any other owner, rest with the Supplier.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that disclosing Proprietary Information does not grant the receiving party any rights to it (including copyrights, etc.). This is changed to indicate that such disclosure *does* grant those rights to the receiving party, contradicting the original statement. Additionally, we change the phrase that the property in all information rest with the Buyer, and instead the property rests with the Supplier. This creates an in-text contradiction because it provides an additional interpretation on the original text, creating an unexpected contradiction that will create uncertainty, and it provides an in-text contradiction with the previous clause, in which the property remains with the disclosing Party. This introduces a perturbation to the original document because there are terms contradicting within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Even if clause 12.2 of the General Purchase Conditions are in place, it is expressly understood and agreed by the Parties hereto that the disclosure and provision of Proprietary Information under this Agreement by a Party to the other Party shall be construed as granting to the receiving Party under the laws of any country rights whether expressed or implied by licence or otherwise on the matters, inventions or discoveries to which such Proprietary Information pertains including any copyright, trademark or trade secret rights. The property in all information and/or data disclosed by the Buyer to the Supplier pursuant to this Agreement shall subject t o any right of any other owner, rest with the Supplier.
perturbation.justification: The original clause stated that disclosure of Proprietary Information does *not* grant rights to the receiving party, and the property remains with the buyer. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that such disclosure *does* grant rights to the receiving party and changes the phrase that the property remains with the supplier, creating an in-text contradiction. This is a clear and direct conflict making the terms inconsistent and ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: 10. The execution, existence and performance of this Agreement shall be kept confidential by the Parties hereto and shall not be disclosed by either Party without the prior written consent of the other.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The execution, existence and performance of this Agreement is not confidential. Either Party can disclose it as they see fit, even without the consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates that the existence and details of the agreement be kept confidential. The revised version explicitly states the opposite: the agreement is *not* confidential and can be disclosed by either party without consent. This introduces a perturbation that contradicts directly to what was previously agreed in the document. This introduces a perturbation to the original document because there is a term contradicting within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. The execution, existence and performance of this Agreement is not confidential. Either Party can disclose it as they see fit, even without the consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement's details must be kept confidential. The changed text explicitly states that the agreement is not confidential and can be disclosed by either party without consent. The contradicted text is in the same place as the Changed Text, which makes the in-text contradiction very strong. The modified text directly conflicts with its original form, creating a clear and undeniable contradiction within the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 11. The end or termination of this Agreement shall not relieve the receiving Party of complying with the obligations imposed by paragraph 4 thereof with respect to the use and protection of the Proprietary Information received prior to the date of termi nation or end of this Agreement. Such obligations shall continue for the period applicable as set forth in said paragraph.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. The end or termination of this Agreement shall relieve the receiving Party of complying with the obligations imposed by paragraph 4 thereof with respect to the use and protection of the Proprietary Information received prior to the date of termi nation or end of this Agreement. Such obligations will cease at the end of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that termination of the agreement does not relieve the receiving party of its confidentiality obligations outlined in paragraph 4. The changed clause states that termination *does* relieve the receiving party of those obligations. This change causes an in-text contradiction because the protection for Proprietary Information is going to cease, which means that the party that received said Proprietary Information, may not be obligated to protect the information, contradicting section 4 of the document. This introduces a perturbation to the original document because there is a term contradicting within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. The receiving Party hereby covenants that, during the validity period of this Agreement and for a period of five (5) years after its end or its termination, the Proprietary Information received from the disclosing Party shall: (a) be protected and kept in strict confidence by the receiving Party which must use the same degree of precaution and safegu ards as it uses to protect its own proprietary information of like importance, but in no case any less than reasonable care, (b) be only disclosed to and used by those persons who have a need to know and solely for the purpose specified in this Agreement, within the receiving Party's organisation and to its professional advisors, provided that in this latter case the disclosing Party so notifies the other Party and obtains the authorization from the other Party to make such disclosure, such authorizat ionnot being unreasonably withheld, and provided that such advisor(s) agree(s) to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement. (Buyer shall be entitled to freely disclose such information to its affiliated companies, to its co -contractors for a project and to its final customer). (c) not be used in whole or in part for any purpose other than the purpose of this Agreement as specified in the preamble and for the Project above without the prior written consent of the disclosing Party, (d) neither be disclosed nor caused to be disclosed whether directly or indirectly to any third Party or persons other than t hose mentioned in subparagraph b) or the Buyer Clients or its co -contractors for the Project, (e) neither be copied, nor otherwise reproduced nor duplicated in whole or in part where such copying, reproduction or duplic ation have not been specifically authorized in writing by the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that termination does not relieve the receiving party of confidentiality obligations. The changed text states that termination does relieve the receiving party of these obligations, which directly contradicts the original agreement in section 4, which states the obligations continue for five years after termination. The change introduces a clear and direct conflict, making enforcement unclear.
